subject: [PATCH v3 1/3] fuse: fix race between registration and connection abortion

2026-06-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a race condition in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module, specifically in the io_uring code. The issue arises when a connection is being aborted while another thread is registering an SQE (Submission Queue Entry). The patch introduces a check for the connection status under a spinlock, ensuring that if the connection has been aborted, the queue reference count will be decremented and the stop waitqueue will be woken up. This prevents a deadlock situation where the abort/unmount thread hangs indefinitely due to leaked resources.
